channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Missão Apoio
item.description: Moreira Franco, former minister and governor, criticized the MDB's alliance as a 'salad' of incoherent agreements and launched a memoir of over a thousand pages revealing behind-the-scenes details of the party's internal crisis. The book contains severe criticisms of the MDB's current leadership, highlighting internal disagreements and lack of political direction. The launch on March 15, 2026, generated repercussions in the Brazilian electoral landscape.

item.title: Zeca Dirceu pede votos de presente de aniversário para o pai
item.description: Deputy Zeca Dirceu (PT-PR) asked for votes for his father, José Dirceu, a pre-candidate for federal deputy from São Paulo, during the latter's 80th birthday celebration in Brasília. He said the most just gift for his father is to strengthen his campaign and ensure his return to Congress, thereby boosting the PT's influence. The event was attended by government officials, highlighting José Dirceu's political reintegration after his Lava Jato-related absence.

item.title: Dirceu diz que Lula não pode ser mais ‘Lulinha paz e amor’, ataca Flávio e lança jingle de campanha
item.description: José Dirceu, former Chief of Staff, called for a combative electoral campaign to re-elect Lula, rejecting the 'Lulinha paz e amor' image and attacking Flávio Bolsonaro as a coup supporter aligned with U.S. interests. He warned of threats to national sovereignty and urged PT mobilization, while celebrating his 80th birthday attended by figures like Vice President Alckmin. Dirceu also announced his bid for a federal deputy seat in 2026, 21 years after his conviction in the mensalão scandal.

item.title: Nova regra de precatórios causa impasse de até R$ 44 bi na meta fiscal de 2027
item.description: A new rule for the payment of court-ordered debts (precatórios) is causing a divergence in interpretation between the government and experts, potentially requiring up to R$44 billion in additional revenues to meet the 2027 fiscal target. The government aims to achieve a primary surplus of 0.5% of GDP, but depending on how the rule is applied, this goal may become unattainable without further budget adjustments.

item.title: Apesar de defesa de Valdemar, PP pisa no freio na articulação para Tereza ser vice de Flávio
item.description: Despite Valdemar Costa Neto, PL leader, expressing preference for Senator Tereza Cristina (MS) as Flávio Bolsonaro's running mate, her party, the PP, is slowing down the articulation. The move reflects internal resistance within the PP to finalize the ticket, despite external support from their ally.

item.title: Entre Lula e Flávio, só um ameaça a democracia: Flávio. É só um fato!
item.description: The article argues that only Flávio Bolsonaro poses a threat to democracy, while Lula defends a genuine democratic project. The author criticizes Flávio's rhetoric, emphasizing his praise for the military dictatorship and rejection of electoral results, despite any moderation discourse. The PT's shift to labeling Flávio as a democratic risk is endorsed as a view already held by the author.

item.title: Agente de Neymar confia em convocação: ‘Ancelotti não abrirá mão do coração do Brasil’
item.description: Neymar's agent Pini Zahavi expressed confidence that Carlo Ancelotti will include the Brazilian star in the 2026 World Cup squad, calling him 'the heart of Brazil'. Although Neymar was omitted from the March friendlies against France and Croatia due to not being at 100% physical fitness, Ancelotti stated the player can still earn a spot if he regains full condition.

item.title: Nauê Bernardo Azevedo toma posse como ministro substituto do TSE na classe dos juristas
item.description: Nauê Bernardo Azevedo was sworn in as a substitute minister of the Superior Electoral Court (TSE) in the jurists' class, replacing Edilene Lôbo. The ceremony was presided over by Minister Cármen Lúcia, who praised his academic background, OAB involvement, and experience in higher courts, emphasizing his expected contribution to the 2026 elections.

item.title: Dirceu diz que Brasil será governado por Trump se Flávio ganhar. Vídeo
item.description: Former minister José Dirceu (PT) warned that if Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) wins the 2026 presidential election, Brazil would be governed by Donald Trump’s interests and the United States. He accused Flávio of aligning with Trump’s side, claiming it threatens national sovereignty and democracy, during his 80th birthday celebration in Brasília.

item.title: Plenário do TSE confirma mandato do prefeito de General Maynard (SE) eleito em 2024
item.description: The TSE confirmed the mandate of General Maynard (SE) mayor Marcones Melo de Souza Santos, elected in 2024, rejecting the claim of ineligibility based on alleged socioaffective ties with former mayor Valmir de Jesus Santos. By a 5-2 vote, the Collegiate overturned the TRE-SE’s decision, which had disqualified the candidacy based on witness testimonies and social media posts. Minister Nunes Marques emphasized that the relationship—nephew by affinity (third degree)—does not fall under the constitutional ineligibility rule.

item.title: Flávio diz que Moraes “ficou de avaliar” prisão domiciliar a Bolsonaro
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) met with Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes to submit a new request for house arrest for former President Jair Bolsonaro, citing the worsening of his health due to bilateral bacterial pneumonia. Flávio stated that Moraes 'will evaluate' the request, indicating the minister will review it based on the former president's medical condition.

item.title: Governo Lula cancela evento do ECA Digital
item.description: The Lula government postponed the ceremony to sign decrees, including the one regulating the ECA Digital, to next Wednesday (18), after the event originally scheduled for today was canceled upon request from authorities. The event, expected to host over 700 guests, aimed to formalize measures protecting children and adolescents in online environments.

item.title: TSE defere registro de candidatura de vereadora eleita em Itabaiana (PB) nas Eleições 2024
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) approved the candidacy registration of Ana Carolina Oliveira de Medeiros, known as Carol de Segundo, a councilwoman elected in Itabaiana (PB) in the 2024 municipal elections, reversing a decision by the Paraíba Regional Electoral Court (TRE-PB) that had denied registration due to alleged lack of timely party affiliation. Reporting minister Nunes Marques emphasized that a social media post by the candidate clearly demonstrates her participation in a MDB party affiliation event within the legal deadline, validating the documentation. The ruling also acknowledged the structural challenges women face entering politics, a field historically dominated by men.

item.title: O pedido de Flávio Bolsonaro a Alexandre de Moraes em reunião no STF
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro, a 2026 presidential pre-candidate, stated he met with Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes to request humanitarian house arrest for his father, Jair Bolsonaro, who is hospitalized with bilateral bacterial pneumonia. He said the meeting was objective and expressed concern over his father's worsening health, while acknowledging he is being well treated in the Papuda prison complex.

item.title: Gleisi sofre derrota para Flávio Bolsonaro em ação na Justiça de Brasília
item.description: The Brasília Justice denied an injunction requested by Minister Gleisi Hoffmann to force Senator Flávio Bolsonaro to remove a social media post claiming the PT 'would protect' crime. The judge ruled the content, though harsh, is protected by freedom of expression and the right to criticism, particularly as it involves public figures with opposing ideologies. The decision does not rule on the merits; the case remains under judicial secrecy and will proceed to evidence production.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro diz que conversou com Moraes sobre prisão domiciliar do pai
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro said he met with Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes to discuss a request for humanitarian house arrest for his father, former President Jair Bolsonaro, who is currently hospitalized at DFStar after health complications while serving time in Papudinha. Flávio argued that the lack of constant medical supervision in prison poses a risk of death, and Moraes said he would evaluate the request at an appropriate time without setting a deadline.

item.title: Flávio diz que se reuniu com Moraes para reforçar pedido de prisão domiciliar de Bolsonaro
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro said he met with Minister Alexandre de Moraes to reinforce the request for house arrest for former President Jair Bolsonaro, arguing that the current detention regime at Papudinha poses a progressive health risk despite adequate care. The defense submitted an updated medical report citing the possibility of new bronchoaspiration episodes similar to the one that led to his recent hospitalization for bacterial pneumonia.

item.title: Eleições 2026: conheça a ordem de votação na urna eletrônica
item.description: On October 4, 2026, over 155 million Brazilian voters will cast ballots for six offices: president, governors, senators, federal deputies, state deputies, and district deputies. Voting will take place from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. Brasília time, with a potential runoff for president and governors on October 25. The electronic voting machine will present the six races in a fixed sequence, as defined by electoral law.

item.title: Zema lança programa de governo em abril, com ex-assessor de Guedes ajudando na parte econômica
item.description: Romeu Zema, governor of Minas Gerais and pré-candidate for President under the Novo party, announced he will launch his government program on April 16 in São Paulo. The event will feature a former aide of Paulo Guedes to assist in drafting the economic section of the plan. Zema is preparing to leave his state government to focus on the 2026 presidential campaign.

item.title: Flávio diz que teve audiência com Moraes para pedido de prisão domiciliar
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro stated today that he had an audience with Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes to discuss the possibility of house arrest for his father, former President Jair Bolsonaro. The statement was made following the meeting, without additional details on the conversation’s content or any judicial decision.

item.title: Temer diz não se arrepender de nomear Moraes: Se não fosse ele, talvez não tivéssemos eleições
item.description: Former Brazilian President Michel Temer defended Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes, stating he has no regrets about appointing him and suggesting that without Moraes' actions, Brazil might not have had elections. Temer rejected claims that Moraes had been overly harsh in cases such as the fake news inquiry and the criminal proceedings related to the coup plot involving former President Jair Bolsonaro.

item.title: Justiça nega pedido de Gleisi para remover vídeo de Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: The Federal Court of Brasília denied an urgent injunction request by Minister Gleisi Hoffmann (PT) to remove a video posted by Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) on Instagram, which allegedly used digitally manipulated media to link her image to illicit acts. The ruling was issued on Tuesday, the 17th, as reported by Metrópoles. The video was claimed to be digitally altered content.

item.title: Serviço público federal perdeu 73 mil pessoas com Temer e Bolsonaro, diz Dweck
item.description: The federal public service lost 73,580 employees between 2016 and 2022 under the governments of Michel Temer and Jair Bolsonaro, according to the MGI. Under the current Lula administration (2023–2026), there was a net gain of 2,835 employees, with 19,381 hires and 16,546 departures. Minister Esther Dweck stated that the recovery remains insufficient and denied claims of public sector bloat, attributing the losses to the 2019 Pension Reform and the lack of public competitions and salary adjustments during the prior period.

item.title: Não há evidências de que Trump exigiu renúncia de Moraes em 24 horas
item.description: There is no evidence that Donald Trump demanded Alexandre de Moraes resign within 24 hours. The claim spread on social media without basis and was not supported by any official statements, interviews, or news coverage in the U.S. or Brazil. UOL Confere verified U.S. government sources, Trump’s social media profiles, and news outlets, finding no record of such an ultimatum.

item.title: Frente do biodiesel defende mistura de 16% no diesel
item.description: The Biodiesel Front, led by Deputy Arnaldo Jardim (Cidadania-SP), advocates increasing biodiesel blend in diesel from 15% to 16% (B16), using the postponement of the CNPE meeting to push the topic onto the agenda. The sector claims it has the installed capacity to support B16 and argues the measure reduces emissions, strengthens domestic production, enhances energy security, and lowers import dependence.

item.title: Temer diz que sem Moraes talvez não houvesse eleições em 2022 e que não se arrepende da indicação
item.description: Former President Michel Temer defended Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes, stating that without Moraes' actions, Brazil might not have held elections in 2022. Temer praised Moraes' legal and personal courage, saying he has no regret over appointing him to the STF in 2017, despite criticism over Moraes' handling of cases like the fake news inquiry and investigations into Bolsonaro's alleged coup plot.

item.title: “Kassab escolheu Ratinho Junior para disputar o Planalto”, diz Bornhausen
item.description: Jorge Bornhausen claims Gilberto Kassab confirmed Ratinho Junior’s pre-candidacy for president under the PSD, although Kassab’s office denies an official decision has been made. Ratinho Junior is considered the internal favorite due to leading polls, longer party affiliation, and internal strength, tying with Lula in second-round scenarios per a Real Time Big Data survey. The PSD’s final decision is expected by March 27 and will shape coalition-building for the 2026 presidential election.

item.title: Visita ao Poder360: Alex Manente
item.description: Deputy Alex Manente, president of Cidadania-SP, visited the Poder360 headquarters in Brasília on Tuesday (March 17, 2026), accompanied by the party’s general secretary, Juliet Matos. During the visit, he discussed the party’s organizational structure and preparations for the 2026 elections. The interview will be published on Thursday (March 19, 2026).

item.title: Petroleiras vão à Justiça contra MP que cria imposto para exportação
item.description: Major oil companies including Shell, Total, PetroRio, and Repsol are preparing legal action against MP 1.340, which imposes a 12% tax on oil exports, arguing it violates the principle of tributary annuality. Petrobras supports the measure, while the IBP is divided. The Lula government defends the tax as a temporary, revenue-raising tool to curb extraordinary profits from global oil price surges.

item.title: PSDB reúne lideranças para se preparar para campanha deste ano
item.description: The PSDB will hold a seminar on March 18 and 19, 2026, bringing together political leaders and experts to discuss proposals and define the party's electoral campaign strategy for this year. The meeting is part of the party's reconstruction effort to strengthen its presence in municipal and state elections.

item.title: Fátima Bezerra desiste do Senado e fica no governo do RN até o fim do mandato
item.description: Rio Grande do Norte Governor Fátima Bezerra (PT) has abandoned her 2026 Senate bid and will remain in office until the end of her term in December. She had initially planned to resign in April to run for the Senate but decided instead to complete her gubernatorial mandate.

item.title: Band e TV Cultura fecham parceria para a realização dos debates presidenciais das Eleições 2026
item.description: Band and TV Cultura have partnered to host the presidential debates for the 2026 elections, with the first debate in August and a second-round runoff interview in October. The collaboration aims to elevate journalistic quality and provide voters with qualified information for a conscious decision. TV Cultura began electoral coverage in January with thematic programs and will continue with initiatives like 'De Olho no Voto' and individual interviews on Roda Viva.

item.title: Com ECA Digital já em vigor, Lula adia cerimônia de assinatura
item.description: The signing ceremony for three decrees regulating the Digital ECA (Child and Adolescent Digital Statute) was postponed to Wednesday afternoon (Mar 18, 2026) for further technical review. Law No. 15.211/2025, which took effect on Tuesday (Mar 17), establishes digital protections for minors, banning self-declared age, loot boxes, advertising profiling, and requiring privacy-by-design settings and parental supervision tools.

item.title: Governadora do RN desiste de renúncia e reforça apoio a secretário para sucessão
item.description: Rio Grande do Norte Governor Fátima Bezerra (PT) announced she will not resign or run for the Senate in 2026, choosing to complete her term until December. She reaffirmed her support for Finance Secretary Cadu Xavier (PT) as her successor, after Vice Governor Walter Alves (MDB) refused to assume the governorship, making a Senate run unfeasible. Bezerra accused Alves of breaking a 2022 agreement and serving the interests of an 'old elite' opposed to PT rule in the state.

item.title: Master pressiona Congresso, que pressiona Alcolumbre, que pressiona Lula, que diz não controlar a PF
item.description: The Banco Master scandal has triggered a blame-shifting cycle among Brazil’s Three Powers, with Congress pressuring Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, who in turn pressures the Lula administration, despite the president denying control over the Federal Police. Lawmakers suspect that banker Daniel Vorcaro may strike a deal with prosecutors or the PF, potentially implicating the centrão, while Alcolumbre resists establishing a CPI and struggles to contain Senate debates. The controversy is damaging Lula’s popularity and could impact the PT in Bahia, particularly after he publicly highlighted Master’s investments in pension funds from Amapá and Rio just before a PF operation in those states.

item.title: Congresso promulga acordo comercial entre Mercosul e UE
item.description: Brazil’s Congress promulgated the EU-Mercosul free trade agreement, finalized after over 25 years of negotiations. The pact covers around 720 million people and a combined GDP exceeding $22.4 trillion, eliminating tariffs on over 90% of bilateral trade. Although subject to a pending review by the EU Court of Justice, the agreement will be provisionally applied starting in May, with Brazil expected to be a major beneficiary.

item.title: Band e TV Cultura fecham acordo para realização de debates à Presidência nas eleições
item.description: Band and TV Cultura have reached an agreement to jointly produce debates with presidential candidates in the 2026 elections. The partnership, negotiated since the beginning of the year, was confirmed on March 17, 2026. The full article is behind a paywall, requiring subscription or access granted by subscribers.

item.title: Lula diz que “mulher não tem dono” ao receber medalhista paralímpico
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) stated that 'women have no owner' during a ceremony at the Planalto Palace, defending gender equality and the valorization of sports, upon receiving Paralympic medalist Cristian Ribera. Lula criticized outdated stereotypes, emphasized the importance of public policies for economically vulnerable athletes, and reinforced sports as a tool for social inclusion and transformation.

item.title: Maioria já tem voto definido para presidente, aponta pesquisa
item.description: The Genial/Quaest poll shows that 56% of Brazilians have already decided on their presidential vote for the October elections, while 43% remain open to changing their choice. The survey interviewed 2,004 people aged 16 and over between March 6 and 9.

item.title: Com julgamento no TSE, Castro avalia saída antecipada
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) is considering an early resignation to avoid impeachment by the TSE after Justice Nunes Marques requested a delay in the case. His legal team argues that resigning before the trial concludes would render the case moot, but experts warn that ineligibility could still apply if his direct involvement in wrongdoing is proven. A second option to step down by April 4 depends on confidence he will avoid both removal and ineligibility, amid growing perceptions that political rivals see him as less antagonistic toward the Supreme Court.

item.title: STF não cassa os mandatos de deputados do PL condenados por desvio em emendas
item.description: The STF ruled against immediately revoking the mandates of PL deputies Bosco Costa, Josimar Maranhãozinho, and Pastor Gil, convicted of embezzlement and bribery in parliamentary amendments, but declared them ineligible. The decision on whether to remove them from office is left to the Chamber of Deputies, as stated by rapporteur Cristiano Zanin. Sentences range from 5 to 6 years and 5 months in semi-open regime, with fines up to 300 day-fines.

item.title: Lula recebe atleta paralímpico no Planalto após medalha inédita
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva received Paralympic athlete Cristian Ribera at the Planalto Palace, who won a silver medal in cross-country skiing at the 2026 Milan-Cortina Winter Paralympics — Brazil’s first-ever medal in the event. Lula praised the athletes’ dedication and announced the government is evaluating measures to reduce or eliminate taxes on sports equipment used by Paralympic athletes, aiming to lower costs and improve access to gear, especially for imported-dependent sports, as part of a broader strategy to strengthen high-performance sports with inclusion and international competitiveness.

item.title: Áudio: Procuradoria da Mulher do Senado lança Guia da Candidata para eleições deste ano
item.description: The Senate's Women's Prosecutor's Office launched the Candidate's Guide to assist women in running for office and navigating political life, offering practical advice from pre-candidacy to post-election. Senator Augusta Brito (PT-CE) emphasized that women face greater obstacles in politics and the guide aims to help change this reality.

item.title: Vorcaro ameaça atingir PT e governo Lula em delação premiada sobre conexões do Master
item.description: Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Banco Master, is negotiating a plea bargain with the PGR to leave prison, aiming to implicate the PT and Lula’s government in the scandal. His ties to the STF, Centrão politicians, and Bahia state officials (Rui Costa and Jaques Wagner) are already under investigation, and he may now point to the PT in Bahia and his former partner Augusto Lima to reduce his liability. However, STF allies warn that selective, politically motivated pleas will not be accepted, as all data will be cross-checked with existing investigations.

item.title: As penas de deputados do PL condenados pelo STF por desvio de emendas
item.description: The First Chamber of the Supreme Federal Court unanimously convicted deputies Josimar Maranhãozinho and Pastor Gil (both PL-MA) and former deputy João Bosco (PL-SE) of passive corruption in the diversion of parliamentary amendments. Sentences range from 5 to 6 years and 5 months in semi-open regime, with fines and a joint payment of R$1.66 million for moral damages. Mandates were not immediately revoked, but the defendants are now ineligible, with the Chamber of Deputies to decide on their continued tenure.

item.title: Fátima Bezerra desiste de disputar o Senado e permanece no governo do RN
item.description: Rio Grande do Norte Governor Fátima Bezerra (PT) withdrew her 2026 Senate candidacy after a split with her vice-governor, Walter Alves (MDB), announcing she will remain in office until her term ends. She endorsed her finance secretary, Cadu Xavier, as her successor, stating no Senate position justifies compromising her integrity and commitment to the state. Allies say the decision followed a request from President Lula, who feared the PT would fail to elect a successor in a potential indirect legislative vote if both she and Alves resigned.

item.title: Vereador anuncia Flávio em São Caetano, mas não combina com pré-candidato
item.description: A councilman announced support for pre-candidate Flávio in São Caetano do Sul, but the announcement was not coordinated or endorsed by the pre-candidate himself, creating a local campaign discrepancy. The episode highlights a lack of alignment between local leaders and the official pre-candidacy strategy, potentially undermining the political group's cohesion.

item.title: Filipe Martins apresenta recurso ao STF e pede anulação de condenação
item.description: Filipe Martins, former advisor to Jair Bolsonaro, filed a petition with the STF seeking to annul his 21-year prison sentence for involvement in the coup plot. His defense claims the Court relied on false evidence from the Federal Police and challenges Mauro Cid’s testimony, while also arguing the STF lacks jurisdiction due to absence of privileged forum.

item.title: Primeira-ministra do Peru renuncia três semanas depois de assumir o cargo e antes das eleições
item.description: Peru's Prime Minister Denisse Miralles resigned three weeks after taking office, just one day before she was scheduled to seek a confidence vote in Congress. Her resignation comes less than a month before the April 12 general elections, further deepening political instability in a country that has seen eight presidents since 2018 due to resignations and removals. The move also coincides with Peru's worst energy crisis in two decades, triggered by a gas pipeline rupture that forced natural gas rationing and disrupted industry.

item.title: Esposa do prefeito Ricardo Nunes oficializa filiação ao MDB
item.description: Regina Carnovale Nunes, wife of São Paulo Mayor Ricardo Nunes, officially joined the MDB party in March 2026, targeting the 2026 elections. She described the move as a 'conscious step' to continue working on social causes, family strengthening, and building a more just and humane city. Her affiliation comes amid a civil inquiry into alleged administrative impropriety involving the mayor and a rented property tied to unlicensed contracts with the city, which yielded R$624 million to a construction firm between 2021 and 2024.
